'''Stribers''' were an aquatic [[Species/Legends|species]] originally native to [[Julsujod III]]. The Stribers relocated to the [[Water/Legends|water]]-covered [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] to escape a cataclysm that struck their homeworld<ref name="gamer1" /> around five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]],<ref name="Date">{{CSWECite|II|119|Iskalonian}} states that the [[Species/Legends|species]] constituting the [[Iskalonian]] cultural group were brought to [[Iskalon/Legends|Iskalon]] approximately five [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] before the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Since "[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Marvel Series]]" establishes that the relocation of the Iskalonian species was prefaced by the cataclysms on the species' original [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworlds]], the cataclysms must also have occurred around that [[Time/Legends|time]].</ref> and became a part of the [[Iskalonian]] [[The School|School]], a cultural group of several aquatic species that had traveled to Iskalon with the aid of [[Unidentified spacefaring benefactors|spacefaring benefactors]]. [[Color/Legends|Blue]]-skinned and [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]], Stribers were known as very slow to react when addressed, as they preferred to carefully examine all aspects of a situation before acting. By the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Stribers were the rarest species among the School, and [[Biologist/Legends|biologists]] estimated that they would die out completely within the next few generations.<ref name="gamer1" />
